codeReview.summary: Documentation-only change adding a 'Team & Shared Memory' section to README.md (45 lines). All 6 review criteria are met: (1) section exists at line 423, (2) contains 3 architecture options (Shared Database, Sync via Wormhole, Per-User Partitioning), (3) includes Privacy Considerations subsection with 5 bullet points, (4) includes Team Setup Checklist with 5 steps, (5) correctly positioned after Multi-Machine Support (line 413) and before Schema (line 468), (6) content is well-written, technically accurate, and complete. Option 1 honestly notes that MEMORY_DB_PATH env var support would need to be added to scripts. Option 2 leverages existing wormhole architecture. Option 3 provides concrete SQL for per-user partitioning. Privacy section covers plaintext storage, machine attribution, sensitive fact risk, curation recommendation, and encryption-at-rest for regulated environments.

issues.description: Option 1 documents a MEMORY_DB_PATH environment variable that doesn't yet exist in the codebase. While the note transparently states this 'requires adding' the support, a reader could initially mistake this for existing functionality. Consider prefixing the code block with '(proposed)' or similar.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No SKILL.md file found for the 'python-worker' skill at .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md. The feature's skillName is 'python-worker' but the skill definition file doesn't exist in the expected location. Cannot verify if the worker followed the skill's procedure since there's no procedure to compare against.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Glob search for **/*SKILL* under .factory/ returned no matches. Feature JSON has skillName: 'python-worker'.

summary: Clean pass. The Team & Shared Memory documentation section is well-structured and meets all 6 review criteria. It adds 45 lines to README.md with three concrete architecture options, privacy considerations, and a team setup checklist. The only minor note is that Option 1 references a proposed (not yet implemented) MEMORY_DB_PATH environment variable, but the documentation is transparent about this. No code changes were made, consistent with the documentation-only requirement. Commit message follows the expected 'docs:' convention.
